headline: Microsoft CFO Amy Hood to step down next year, succession plan underway
body: Microsoft Corp said on Friday that long-tenured Chief Financial Officer Amy Hood will retire next year. The company has begun a formal succession process. Hood, who has served as CFO since 2013, was widely credited with the disciplined messaging around the AI capex cycle that has weighed on operating margins. Shares were down 2% in after-hours trading. The CEO Satya Nadella said in a statement that the firm has identified several internal candidates and is also conducting an external search.
